paper_title: SGP Database and Data Collection Methods

methods.description: The process of implementing and designing a relational database using PostgreSQL for storing geological and geochemical data.

steps.description: SGP utilizes a relational database implemented with the PostgreSQL database management system.
steps.searchable_summary: implement a relational database using PostgreSQL

steps.description: Tables for analytical geochemistry are from the British Geological Survey (BGS) geochemistry data model, with minor modifications.
steps.searchable_summary: adapt BGS geochemistry data model for database tables

steps.description: Tables for geological, geographical, and sample details are based on established museum collection management databases.
steps.searchable_summary: use museum collection management databases for table design

methods.description: The method of storing analytical data in their original format and handling detection limits in the database.

steps.description: We store analytical data in their submitted or published format and do not standardize the results to any given unit.
steps.searchable_summary: store analytical data in original format without standardization

steps.description: An analytical result may be empty (NULL) only if it is below or above detection limits, and those values are also stored if they are available.
steps.searchable_summary: handle NULL values for detection limits in data storage

methods.description: The process of collecting data from researchers and integrating them into the SGP database.

steps.description: The SGP welcomes contributions from any interested researchers.

methods.description: The process of archiving and describing the Phase 1 data collection, including error correction and public release.

steps.description: A static version of the database was archived and made available to collaborators through the website and via tailored queries.
steps.searchable_summary: archive static version of database and provide access

steps.description: Time was allowed for vetting, and any errors discovered were corrected before the final freeze.
steps.searchable_summary: vet and correct errors in database before final freeze

methods.description: The method of presenting and providing access to the Phase 1 database through a search website with an API.

steps.description: The SGP search website utilizes an intuitive user interface to query the Phase 1 database via an API.
steps.searchable_summary: develop user interface for querying database via API

steps.description: Data are converted to one standard unit, and oxides are converted to elements.
steps.searchable_summary: convert data to standard units and elements
